entry.title: Accounting document not offsettable with CME 1
entry.content:
I'm currently facing a situation regarding postings with CME 1 for clients.

In our case, we've been using CME 1 to post deposits received from our clients (posted to account 1800000 as a CME reconciliation account, leaving the entry open in the client's account as a special ledger entry).

Now, our client wants to offset a deposit with an invoice. When they go to the "Offset Payments Received" application, the option to offset these items is not available. Is there a configuration setting to modify this?

 

Additionally, and what is most striking, if I go to the "automatic payment management" app it does allow me to access these items, since I have configuration point 101293 with CME 1

I cannot use the automatic payment management app because I have to apply the difference between the deposit and the invoice to the customer's account and leave it pending. Its the reason

Is this about the special ledger transaction type? Does it mean that all entries that are "Z" cannot be offset?

Thank for your help!!

entry.title: Intangible assets
entry.content: How to set Intangible assets in the Fixed asset master data ,as Items or Itemised Services

entry.title: Revaluation of Fixed Asset – Posting in a Separate Document
entry.content:
Currently, the revaluation of assets is posted in the AFAB document during the depreciation run, and the amounts are compressed based on the GL accounts.

Would it be possible to post the revaluation of fixed assets in a separate document and for each asset individually? Can this be customized? This is a legal requirement in Turkey for tax regulations.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Unable to Revert Fiscal Year Variant (SZ) from Calendar Year to Year-Dependent
entry.content:
Dear Experts, 

Good day.

I am currently configuring the Fiscal Year Variant in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using the standard variant SZ (Configurable Template Variant). I would like to maintain a year-dependent fiscal year variant.

Initially, I was able to successfully set up the SZ variant as year-dependent and define the custom fiscal year variant accordingly. However, during testing, I changed the setting to Calendar Year (by selecting the relevant checkbox).

After making this change, I am now unable to revert the configuration back to year-dependent. When attempting to do so, I encounter the following error message:

  • "Deletion not possible for record created by SAP Message no. /CFG/CF_SSC_FRW123"

Actually, my intention was not to delete any SAP pre-delivered configuration. Also, the system currently does not contain any journal entries or postings. Based on my understanding, it should still be possible to adjust the fiscal year variant at this stage.

May I kindly seek further advice on whether there is any recommended approach to revert the configuration back to year-dependent?

Thank you very much in advance for your support.

Best regards, 

HY

entry.title: cached document type stored when using different fixed assets tcodes
entry.content: After performing an FA transaction using one of the t code for example AUC settlement, then performing another transaction for example transfer within company code, the document type is auto populated as the previous doc type ie AU. Is there a way to prevent the system from defaulting the document type to the previously used ones?

entry.title: CAPEX BUDGET PR/PO LEVEL PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content:
CAPEX Budgeting,

In SAP Public Cloud, I want to set a budget at asset class-wise, read all blogs and posts, but not get an exact answer or process flow.
My client is using PR to initiate CAPEX. When I use Account assignment A, I have no option to give WBS Element; without that, I know no budget will be consumed. If I use assignment P, then SAP asks for WBS and GL account, and that GL account then appears DR. Given GL A/C and CR GRIR account, that is not required 

If I manually give AUC Asset GL, then SAP does not consume the budget i believe this is due to the nature of GL to work with WBS; we need a cost element, not B/S GL
how i set budget on PR/PO level for CAPEX

entry.title: [FI-AA]asset history sheet not matching with moment type report.
entry.content: While we are using t code - S_ALR_87011990 - Asset History Sheet, we want to know from which table this report is picking the values and how the GL transaction type and movement types are linked with asset transactions like acquisitions, retirement and transfers in which table these link is available.

entry.title: asset history sheet not matching with moment type report.
entry.content: While we are using t code - S_ALR_87011990 - Asset History Sheet, we want to know from which table this report is picking the values and how the GL transaction type and movement types are linked with asset transactions like acquisitions, retirement and transfers in which table these link is available.

entry.title: Parallel API Execution for Fixed Asset Processing Across Multiple Company Codes
entry.content:
Current Situation

We are investigating whether it is possible to execute depreciation expense allocation for fixed assets via API for multiple company codes in parallel.

At the moment, our processing is executed sequentially in the following order:

  • Company A data → API processing completed
  • Company B data → API processing completed

To improve performance, the customer is considering changing this to parallel execution, where multiple company codes are processed simultaneously via API.

Current Concern

We would like to understand the technical implications of sending multiple API requests in parallel, especially when:

  • Multiple company codes are processed at the same time
  • The same fixed asset (or related asset data) might be updated concurrently
  • Requests are triggered via middleware (e.g., SAP Integration Suite / CPI)

We could not find clear documentation in SAP Help, SAP Notes, or API documentation regarding parallel execution behavior for this scenario.

Questions

  1. Is parallel API execution supported for fixed asset processing across multiple company codes?
    (e.g., depreciation expense allocation or similar asset-related updates)
  2. If parallel execution is possible, are there any official SAP documents, API guides, best practices, or restrictions that describe this behavior?
  3. If multiple parallel API requests update the same fixed asset at nearly the same time, can this cause:
    • lock conflicts,
    • update conflicts,
    • or failed updates?
  4. If anyone has implemented a similar process, could you please share:
    • the API/service used,
    • whether parallel execution worked,
    • any issues encountered,
    • and how those issues were avoided?

Expected Outcome

We would appreciate any:

  • official SAP references (SAP Help, Notes, KBAs)
  • real project experience
  • recommendations for safe design

Thank you very much for your support.

entry.title: When executing FBL1N, the user data in the document header text field is not displayed
entry.content:
Hi SAP support,
When executing FBL1N  in Traditional Chinese mode, the user data in the document header text field is not displayed, but the user data can be displayed correctly after selecting the layout again.

There is no issue in English mode, the user data is displayed correctly when executing FBL1N.

 

Best Regards,

Sharlin

entry.title: Billing Condition Type to GL Account CDS view for creating custom sales register in sap public cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to create a custom report for Sales register. Which contains the billing document item details along with the condition type and the GL used to post the condition amount.

I am planning to develop this in the Eclipse.

I have tried to achieve the field by joining multiple CDS view as below. But unable to get the expected result.

I_BillingDocumentItem
I_BillingDocumentItemPrcgElmnt
I_SalesDocument
I_OperationalAcctgDocItem

If anyone from the community please help me to achieve this requirement.

entry.title: Document type is auto defaulted after performing an asset transaction before
entry.content: After performing AUC settlement and trying to perform a transfer of asset within company code afterward, the document type is auto defaulted to the previously performed transaction ie AU(AUC settlement). Is there a way to not have this field auto defaulted specific to asset related transactions?

entry.title: Partial payments/invoice for 1 purchased pc
entry.content:
Hi all,

If you have a PO for 1 item, 1 PC to be manufactured, but Sourcing negotiated partial payments according to some milestones (some before receiving that item physically), e.g., 30% upon PO issuance, 30% upon engineering approval, and 40% upon receipt of the material.

How should we proceed in this case? In my opinion, a GR should only be done when the material arrives to the warehouse and therefor just then received in SAP... per my opinion, you shouldn't receive 30% or 40% of what you haven't physically receive... a stock count/control would be having problems too... what about Source to pay here? and what about the controls that indicates to Finance they can pay the negotiated percentage? Finance usually likes to have GR as back up of payments.... how does it work in this regard when if in the PO the "installment" payment terms in the PO is selected? and what is the link in these cases with GR in these cases?
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Cross-Plant Actual Cost Roll-up
entry.content:
We are facing a question regarding GROW standard capabilities and will appreciate your insights.

Business Scenario

  • We have Plant A and Plant B.
  • Plant A manufactures a semifinished product.
  • Plant B procures this semifinished product from Plant A to assemble the Finished Product.

Question

After running CKMLCP, can we achieve a Cross-Plant Actual Cost Roll-up in system?  Can we see cost break down from Plant B?

entry.title: G/L Account Short Description showing On Inputs and Outputs
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

In the Transactions are Reports for G/L accounts like ( Manage Journal entries  and Display Line items in General Ledgers )  we found Short Descriptions of Ledger accounts is only showing is there for Input selection and Journal entry display. Is there  any work around to Make those description into long description as a standard to Avoid Collapse of identification between accounts Here i have attached the sample screenshots for your reference Kindly provide a quick solution to meet our requirements

entry.title: Clarification on OAYR posting rule setup.
entry.content:
We are currently in New GL and New Asset Accounting migration Phase 2 testing. We have observed that there are posting rule entries in OAYR for group currency depreciation areas 81 and 49 which are created newly. We never did this setup manually.

Could you please review and confirm why these entries exist and what the functional or accounting impact is?

entry.title: DMEE Intermediary Agent - How to add a City Field
entry.content: Is their a Structure field for City (TwnNm) that can be used in the Intermediary Agent tag (/PstlAdr/TwnNm).  Country has been added and is being pulled from PFAYH BNKS1, but I'm not seeing a field for City.  How are others adding this for the ISO 20022 requirements change?

entry.title: Accumulated values for activation date not allowed in transfer year
entry.content:
Dear team,


While creating a legacy asset in AS91 with the following details:

  • Asset Number: 1200159
  • Company Code: 1301

However, while posting values in ABDLT, we are getting the below error.

We would like to create a legacy sub-asset in AS91. However, the sub-asset option is not appearing on the screen.

Could you please help us understand the reason for this issue and assist us in resolving it?
Also, please guide us on how to create a sub-asset through AS91.

Best regards,
Kishore

entry.title: We are unable to settle the previous year’s AUC asset to the cost center
entry.content:
Dear Team,

It has been identified that the previous year’s AUC asset could not be settled to the designated cost center. The system is displaying the following error messages:

  • "Settlement of old asset data to receiver type CTR not allowed"
  • Message Class: AW
  • Message Number: 607

We have reviewed the SAP Notes (3621822 and 120253) suggested for this issue and followed the recommended steps. However, the same error persists.

Could you please suggest and provide a solution, For your reference, we have attached the relevant screenshot.

entry.title: Bypassing Commitment Item Check for Certain Transactions
entry.content:
Hi all, 

I would like for transactions that are posted through the cash journal FBCJ not to be checked for commitment items for the affected accounts. Currently the system is issuing an error that No commitment item entered in item 00001 1001. The GL accounts to be updated have a commitment item but somehow the error is still persisting. I have also traced the derivation rule, the commitment item is being correctly assigned from GL. However, the error is still being issued.

Please help!
